title: Dreaming River Clan

{{Quote|We're too late. It looks like the Dreaming River witches are being rounded up. Those that don't join the Nightsisters will be fed to the rancors.|Kir Kanos, on the capture of the Dreaming River Clan by the Nightsisters and Imperial forces|<ref name="Nightsaber" />}}
The '''Dreaming River Clan''' was a clan of [[Witches of Dathomir|Dathomiri witches]] who lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Around [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], Dathomir was [[Occupation of Dathomir|occupied]] by the [[Imperial Remnant]], a government opposed to the Republic. The Imperials entered into a [[Project Nightsaber|pact]] with [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]], a sect of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] Dathomiri [[Witch/Legends|witches]], and the combined Imperial and Nightsister forces [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] the majority of the [[Dreaming River/Legends|Dreaming River]] Clan. The captive [[The Force/Legends|Force]] witches were subsequently liberated from Imperial internment by a group of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]].

==Description==
The [[Dreaming River/Legends|Dreaming River]] Clan was a tribe of [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-using [[Witches of Dathomir|Dathomiri witches]] that lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]],<ref name="Nightsaber" /> in the [[Quelii sector/Legends|Quelii sector]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The clan dwelled at an encampment in an isolated Dathomir [[Water/Legends|river]] valley and during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the [[Witch/Legends|witch]] [[Astrinol Hja]] acted as their leader. The Dreaming River witches had a matriarchal society and an aversion to being led by [[Gender/Legends|men]], though they were otherwise willing to form alliances with males.<ref name="Nightsaber" />

==History==
{{Quote|There's a slim chance that some of the Dreaming River witches have evaded capture for the moment. They were the closest thing Dathomir had to an underground resistance.|Kir Kanos, on the capture of the Dreaming River Clan by the Nightsisters and Imperial forces|<ref name="Nightsaber" />}}
Circa [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], Dathomir was [[Occupation of Dathomir|occupied]] by the forces of the [[Imperial Remnant]], a [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government opposed to the New Republic. To assist their occupation, the Imperials entered into an [[Project Nightsaber|alliance]] with the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]], a sect of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] Dathomiri witches that opposed the planet's other witch clans. As a gesture of good faith to the Nightsisters, the Imperials rounded up New Republic personnel on the world and members the [[Singing Mountain Clan]], a leading group of Dathomiri witches. The Dreaming River Clan opposed the occupation and formed somewhat of an underground resistance movement to combat the Imperial presence on Dathomir.<ref name="Nightsaber" />

[[File:Dathomir.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Dathomir, the world on which the Dreaming River Clan resided]]
The Dreaming River Clan eventually caught the attention of the Imperial invaders and a combined force of [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial troops]] and [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]]-mounted Nightsisters descended upon the clan's encampment. The majority of the Dreaming River Force witches, including Astrinol Hja, were subsequently arrested. The former [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Imperial Guardsman]] [[Kir Kanos]] suspected that the captured witches would be compelled to join the Nightsisters and would be [[Foodstuff/Legends|fed]] to the Nightsisters' rancors if they refused.<ref name="Nightsaber" />

Kir Kanos<ref name="Nightsaber" /> and group of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]—<ref name="Nightsaber player characters">In ''[[Nightsaber]]'', the player characters roleplay as [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Agent/Legends|agents]] and their identities are dependent on the choices of the players. The entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' for [[Astrinol Hja]] refers to the individuals who rescued her as "[[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]." This article therefore assumes that the actions attributed to the player characters in ''Nightsaber'' were completed by a group of Jedi Knights.</ref> [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-using]] defenders of peace and justice—arrived at the Dreaming River clans' encampment amid the Imperial assault. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knights<ref name="Nightsaber player characters" /> had become stranded on Dathomir and Kanos sought to introduce them to the Dreaming River Clan to seek the witches' assistance in repairing<ref name="Nightsaber" /> the Knights'<ref name="Nightsaber player characters" /> [[Starship/Legends|starship]] so that<ref name="Nightsaber" /> the Jedi<ref name="Nightsaber player characters" /> could leave the world while evading the Imperials.<ref name="Nightsaber" />

{{RPG|''[[Nightsaber]]''}}
The Jedi Knights<ref name="Nightsaber player characters" /> saved several Dreaming River Clan witches from the Nightsisters and Imperial forces. The rescued Force witches agreed to an alliance with<ref name="Nightsaber" /> the Jedi<ref name="Nightsaber player characters" /> and suggested that they send a request for support to [[Teneniel Djo]], a witch of the [[Singing Mountain (Dathomir)/Legends|Singing Mountain]] Clan who was [[Marriage/Legends|married]] to [[Isolder]], a [[Prince/Legends|prince]] of the powerful [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]]. The remaining Dreaming River witches were escorted to an [[Dathomir Imperial Prison|Imperial prison facility]], where they were interred under guard by Nightsisters and Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]].<ref name="Nightsaber" />

With assistance from the rescued witches,<ref name="Nightsaber" /> the Jedi Knights<ref name="Nightsaber player characters" /> infiltrated an Imperial-held [[Dathomir communications center|communications center]] and sent a transmission to the [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]] Consortium asking for help.<ref name="Nightsaber" /> The Jedi Knights<ref name="Nightsaber player characters" /> and Force witches also assaulted the Imperial [[Prison/Legends|prison]] facility where the other Dreaming River witches had been taken and defeated the guards. The captive witches,<ref name="Nightsaber" /> including Astrinol Hja,<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|45|Hja, Astrinol}}</ref> were then liberated from Imperial imprisonment.<ref name="Nightsaber" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Dreaming River Clan was created by [[J.D. Wiker]] and appeared in ''[[Nightsaber]]'', a [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] which was published on [[Wizards.com]] in [[2003]].<ref name="Nightsaber" /> The clan was later mentioned in the [[2008]] compendium ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', in the entry for Astrinol Hja.<ref name="CSWE" />

In ''Nightsaber'', the players roleplay as the Jedi [[Agent/Legends|agents]] stranded on Dathomir and various different outcomes are possible depending on the actions and decisions of the players. During the Imperial and Nightsister attack on the Dreaming River Clan encampment, it is possible for the player characters to rescue a few Dreaming River witches who are isolated from the main group. The witches that they save can then enter an alliance with the player characters and assist them with attacking the communications center and Imperial prison. If the player characters decided to focus on tackling other foes rather than attacking the Imperial prison themselves, their Force witch allies volunteer to raid the prison and rescue their sisters. The witches then sustain heavy casualties in the attack but succeed at freeing most of the Dream River Clan captives.<ref name="Nightsaber" /> ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' confirms that the Jedi rescue Astrinol Hja, indicating the player characters' involvement in the attack to be the official outcome within [[Star Wars]] Legends continuity.<ref name="CSWE" />

The name of the clan is spelled differently in ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia''<ref name="CSWE" /> than to in ''Nightsaber''; in ''Nightsaber'' they are referred to as the "Dreaming River clan"<ref name="Nightsaber" /> whereas in ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' their name is spelled "Dreaming River Clan." This article uses the latter capitalization, because ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' is the most recently published of the two sources.<ref name="CSWE" />
